channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Proposals to cut down on bad driving cross political lanes in Minnesota session
item.description: A variety of bills that aim to make Minnesota’s roads less treacherous are rolling their way through the legislative process. It could wind up as one area of bipartisan consensus in a session where that is a paramount.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration tariffs create uncertainty for brewery owner
item.description: Cary said Begyle Brewing imports about 200K pounds of barley from Canada each year and that tariffs would hurt the brewery's margins.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: EU announces a $5 billion investment in South Africa as the tariffs war with Trump escalates
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — European Union leaders announced 4.7 billion euros ($5.1 billion) in investments in South Africa on Thursday to support green energy and vaccine production, and agreed to start talks on new trade deals with Africa's most advanced economy.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge rules Trump must reinstate many fired federal employees
item.description: A federal judge ruled Thursday that the Trump administration must reinstate probationary government employees fired unlawfully at several agencies, lambasting the Justice Department at a hearing for a “sham” gambit that enabled a key official to avoid testifying in the case. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Activist Mahmoud Khalil's arrest 'deeply concerning': Ex-NYC mayor
item.description: "You don't get arrested for disagreeing with the U.S. government," said former NYC Mayor Bill de Blasio.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Democrat trying again to secure discounts for all Nevadans on 10 expensive prescription medications
item.description: Prescription medication costs are forcing some hard choices for many Nevadans. Two years ago, a proposal that offered hope sailed through the Democrat-majority Nevada Legislature only to be vetoed by Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Red light cameras coming to Las Vegas? Lawmaker wants exception that would allow them
item.description: Traffic cameras to catch drivers in the act of running red lights would be allowed under a bill introduced Tuesday at the Nevada Legislature.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: EPA signals it could narrow Clean Water Act protections
item.description: The law requires the EPA to protect so-called “waters of the United States” but there has been significant political back-and-forth.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: Windsor Park deadlines loom, construction yet to start decades after homes began to sink
item.description: About $37 million is allocated to build 93 new homes for people in Windsor Park, a North Las Vegas neighborhood that began sinking decades ago. Now as deadlines are looming, lawmakers learn that construction has yet to begin.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Minnesota House is tied after DFL wins special election. Here’s what changes
item.description: DFLer David Gottfried won the suburban Ramsey County seat and is expected to be sworn in to the House of Representatives next week.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: 56 percent disapprove of Trump handling of economy: Survey
item.description: The survey comes as Trump faces criticism for his tariff policies and as the stock market has seen significant dips amid concerns of a recession.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Dems under pressure as shutdown fight shifts to Senate
item.description: In the Senate, the bill will need support from at least eight Democrats to get it to President Donald Trump’s desk.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Inflation slows in February ahead of tariff impact
item.description: The latest consumer price index report marks the first indication of inflation trends under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: The European Union retaliates after the U.S. metals tariffs take hold
item.description: The European Union on Wednesday announced retaliatory trade action with a series of duties on U.S. products, responding to the Trump administration rise in tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump's approval dips amid concerns over economy: Poll
item.description: President Donald Trump’s approval rating has dropped a few points since he first took office, with voters increasingly expressing concerns over the state of the economy, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: Education Department says 60 universities under investigation for antisemitism
item.description: The Department of Education’s Office of Civil Rights (OCR) is investigating 60 universities for antisemitism, according to letters sent on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he’s buying ‘brand new’ Tesla to support Musk
item.description: President Donald Trump said he would be buying a “brand new” Tesla to support his senior adviser Elon Musk, as the stock market shows shares of the electric car company tumbling.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Florida seeks easier voting access for hurricane-displaced citizens
item.description: Last year's hurricanes displaced thousands of Floridians, turning their lives upside down and making it even harder for some to vote during a crucial election year. Now, a Pinellas County state lawmaker wants to make it easier for people to cast their ballots after a storm.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida bill would require some elementary school students to learn cursive
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — A new Florida bill would require some elementary school students to learn cursive writing. The bill, HB 921, would require all public schools to provide cursive writing instruction to students in grades 2 through 5.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US added to human rights watchlist over Trump moves
item.description: The United States has been added to CIVICUS's monitor list of potential human rights threats due to President Trump's restrictive executive orders and institutional cutbacks, which are creating an atmosphere to chill democratic dissent.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: Republicans worry Trump's tariffs could harm economy
item.description: Republican lawmakers are growing alarmed over signs that President Trump’s expanding trade war is hurting the economy, something they’re hearing from constituents at home who are struggling to adapt to Trump’s zigzagging tariff pronouncements.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about Mark Carney, elected next Canadian prime minister
item.description: Mark Carney, a former central banker, won his Liberal Party's election in a landslide on Sunday to replace Justin Trudeau as prime minister of Canada. He will be sworn into office in the coming days.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Ras Baraka has highest favorability among Democratic candidates for NJ governor: poll
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) – New polling from Fairleigh Dickinson University gave us some early insight on who voters are looking into in the race for New Jersey governor. Democratic voters weighed in on the candidates' favorability.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11
